meta.title: F15 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW X5 F15 engine — N57, N55, N63, S63 V8. Real UK costs, reliability data and common failures. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X5 F15 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned F15 engine typically costs £3,200–£15,000 supply only, depending on engine — the N57 diesel sits at £4,500–£7,500, while the N63 V8 runs £6,000–£11,000 and the S63 in the X5 M can exceed £15,000. Fitting adds £650–£1,500. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW X5 F15 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The N55B30A (sDrive35i/xDrive35i) — BMW's twin-scroll turbo straight-six with none of the N63's hot-vee complexity. It's the most reliable petrol engine in the F15 range. The N57 diesel (xDrive30d) is a close second — proven and durable with proper maintenance.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X5 F15?

acceptedAnswer.text: N63 V8 valve stem seal and cooling system failures (xDrive50i), N57 diesel injector and EGR issues (xDrive30d/40d/M50d), N55 cooling system and oil leak issues (35i), and — unrelated to the engine — F15 air suspension system failures (all xDrive models).

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW X5 F15 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends heavily on which engine you choose. The N55 straight-six and N57 straight-six diesel are both reliable and proven. The N63 V8 is a glorious engine but has well-documented hot-vee issues — budget for valve-stem-seal and cooling system maintenance. The tri-turbo M50d adds significant complexity. The air suspension system is a common failure point across all models.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW X5 F15 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: The N63 V8 (xDrive50i) — it has well-documented valve-stem-seal and cooling system issues that are expensive to fix. BMW faced a class action over N63 oil consumption. The tri-turbo N57 (M50d) is also one to avoid unless you're prepared for the complexity and cost of maintaining three turbos. If you want the most trouble-free F15, buy an xDrive30d (N57) or xDrive35i (N55).

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my F15 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Blue smoke on startup (N63 valve stem seals), coolant loss or overheating (N63, N55), rough running or loss of power (N57 injectors), or persistent engine warning lights are the key warning signs. For the air suspension, listen for a noisy compressor or notice the car sitting unevenly. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW X5 F15 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a well‑kept £25,000 F15, a £6,500 N63 recon is ~26% of the car's value — safe. Against a tired £14,000 example, the same recon brushes our 70% BMW threshold — walk away. Also factor in the condition of the air suspension system — this can be a significant additional cost.

mainEntity.name: How long do BMW X5 F15 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The N57 diesel can exceed 200,000 miles with proper servicing — regular oil changes (10,000-mile intervals) are essential. The N55 petrol six is similarly durable. The N63 V8's lifespan depends on valve-stem-seal and cooling system maintenance — with proper care, it can reach 150,000+ miles. The S63 in the X5 M is the most fragile — rod bearings are the limiting factor.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the F15 and the previous E70 X5?

acceptedAnswer.text: The F15 (2013–2018) is the third-generation X5 with a lighter, more aerodynamic design, the introduction of sDrive (rear-wheel drive), and updated engines (including the first X5 with a four-cylinder diesel in the sDrive25d). The E70 (2006–2013) is the second generation with older N-series engines and a heavier, bulkier design. The F15 is significantly more efficient and refined — see the E70 Generation Page for that model's specifics.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW X5 F15 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; block stamping is visible from below on N47/B47/N57/N55/N63/S63 units. Tell us your variant (sDrive25d, xDrive25d, xDrive30d, xDrive40d, M50d, sDrive35i, xDrive35i, xDrive50i, xDrive40e, X5 M) and year and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted BMW specialists will confirm it for free.

hero.h1: BMW X5 F15 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the third-generation X5 F15 — from the N47 four-cylinder diesel to the N57 six-cylinder and the N63 V8 that powered the xDrive50i, plus the tri-turbo M50d and S63 V8 in the X5 M. Real UK replacement costs, honest failure data, and the N63 hot-vee issues that still haunt high-mileage V8 cars. Backed by 288+ real UK F15 enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: BMW X5 F15 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the third-generation X5 F15 — from the entry-level four-cylinder diesel to the range-topping V8 and V8-powered X5 M.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: All reliability ratings are [PROVISIONAL] — no dedicated Engine Page exists yet for any F15 engine family. Ratings are derived from cross-referencing specialist and owner sources. The N55 straight-six is widely considered the most reliable petrol option. The N57 diesel sixes are robust but require diligent oil change intervals. The N63 V8 has well-documented hot-vee issues. The tri-turbo N57 in the M50d adds significant complexity. The N20 PHEV is the rarest F15 engine.

overview.intro: The F15 is the third generation of the BMW X5, unveiled at the 2013 Frankfurt International Motor Show and produced from April 2013 to July 2018. It succeeded the E70 with a lighter, more aerodynamic design and introduced sDrive — rear-wheel drive — to the X5 range for the first time. The engine range spanned from the 2.0-litre N47/B47 four-cylinder diesel to the 3.0-litre N57 six-cylinder diesels (including the tri-turbo M50d), the N55 and N63 petrols, the N20 plug-in hybrid, and the S63 V8 in the X5 M. A mid-cycle update in 2015 brought the B47 diesel replacing the N47 in the sDrive25d.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW L4 (shared with F16 X6, F10/F11 5 Series) • Predecessor: BMW X5 E70 (2006–2013) • Successor: BMW X5 G05 (2018–present) • Sibling body styles in this generation: F16 X6 (coupé SUV)
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The F15 X5 generated 288 real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED], concentrated on the N57 diesel and N63 V8 petrol units as these luxury SUVs age out of warranty and maintenance costs become a significant consideration.

items.quote: The N57 is BMW's proven straight-six diesel — 258 bhp, 560 Nm of torque, and genuine 40+ MPG economy in a two-tonne SUV. It's the most balanced engine in the F15 range, delivering effortless performance without the tri-turbo complexity of the M50d. Regular oil changes at 10,000-mile intervals are the key to long life.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want diesel torque, six-cylinder refinement, and proven reliability.

items.quote: The N55 is BMW's twin-scroll turbo straight-six — 306 bhp, 400 Nm of torque, and none of the N63's hot-vee complexity. It's the most reliable petrol engine in the F15 range, delivering smooth, linear power that transforms the X5 into a genuine driver's SUV. No valve-stem-seal issues, no V8 cooling system nightmares.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want petrol refinement and proven straight-six reliability without V8 running costs.

items.quote: The N63 V8 is a glorious engine — 450 bhp, 650 Nm of torque, and a soundtrack that defines the X5 experience. But it carries BMW's 'hot-vee' design, where the turbos sit inside the V of the engine, generating extreme heat. Valve stem seals harden and cause blue smoke on startup; cooling system components fail prematurely. BMW faced a class action over N63 oil consumption issues.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want V8 power and are prepared for V8 maintenance costs.

items.quote: The S63 is BMW's high-performance V8 — 575 bhp, 750 Nm of torque, making it the most powerful engine ever developed for an all-wheel-drive BMW at the time. It shares the N63's hot-vee architecture but adds even more power and stress. Rod bearing wear, turbocharger failure, and cooling system issues are well-documented. A full engine replacement can exceed £15,000.
items.whoItsFor: collectors who want the ultimate F15 X5 and have the budget to maintain it.

items.quote: The xDrive30d delivers 258 bhp, 560 Nm of torque, and genuine 40+ MPG economy — the sweet spot in the F15 range. It's the most common diesel, the most proven, and the cheapest to maintain. No tri-turbo complexity, no V8 fuel bills, and an engine that's proven to exceed 200,000 miles with proper servicing.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want the best all-round F15 without the V8's running costs or the M50d's complexity.

items.quote: The post-2015 sDrive25d replaced the N47 with the B47 — a significant reliability improvement with the timing chain relocated to the front of the engine. It's the most efficient F15 engine — 231 bhp, genuine 45+ MPG, and rear-wheel drive for lighter weight. It's often overlooked in favour of the six-cylinder diesels, but it's a genuine alternative for buyers who don't need all-wheel drive.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want the most efficient F15 and don't need xDrive.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Risk vs Reward — N47 chain risk applies, post-2015 B47 is safer

economicsRule.title: The F15 rule
economicsRule.text: A £6,000–£11,000 N63 recon against a £12,000–£30,000 F15 sits at 20–92% of the car's value. On a sub-£15,000 high-mileage example, that brushes our 70% BMW threshold — we'll honestly tell you to walk away. Against a well-kept £25,000+ example, the same recon drops to a routine 24–44% — a repair decision, not a financial one. Value the car first, and always check service history on V8 and tri-turbo cars. Air suspension is a common additional cost on F15 models.

keyTakeaways.question: Which F15 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: xDrive30d (N57) — proven six-cylinder diesel, strong economy, no V8 or tri-turbo complexity.

keyTakeaways.question: Which F15 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: X5 M (S63 V8) — V8 running costs, rod bearing risk, specialist-only maintenance.

keyTakeaways.question: Which F15 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: xDrive35i (N55) — the best petrol engine in the range, strong residuals, the one enthusiasts want.

problems.title: N63 V8 Valve Stem Seal & Cooling System Failure
problems.rootCause: The N63's 'hot-vee' design — turbos positioned inside the V of the engine — generates significant heat that accelerates wear on valve stem seals. The seals harden and lose their sealing ability, allowing oil to seep past and into the combustion chamber. The classic symptom is a puff of blue smoke on cold startup. BMW faced a class action over this oil consumption defect, which affects the N63 and its predecessors. The cooling system (water pump, thermostat, expansion tank) also fails prematurely due to the extreme under-bonnet temperatures.
problems.recommendation: If your N63 V8 smokes on startup, budget for valve-stem-seal replacement immediately — a £1,500–£3,000 repair is dramatically cheaper than the £6,000+ replacement that follows if the catalytic converters are destroyed or the engine overheats. Regular oil changes (5,000-mile intervals) with high-quality oil are essential.

cta.label: Compare N63 engine prices for your F15 xDrive50i →

problems.title: N57 Diesel Injector & EGR Issues
problems.rootCause: The N57 diesel suffers from injector failure, which can cause rough idling, poor fuel economy, and increased emissions. EGR valve problems and turbocharger failures are also common. The M50d's tri-turbo setup adds additional complexity and potential failure points. High-mileage N57 engines can also develop cracks in the cylinder head.
problems.recommendation: Have the injectors and EGR system inspected at every major service — a £350–£800 preventative service is dramatically cheaper than the £3,500+ replacement that follows if injector failure causes cylinder damage. Use high-quality diesel and change oil every 10,000 miles.

cta.label: Compare N57 engine prices for your F15 xDrive30d/40d/M50d →

problems.title: N55 Cooling System & Oil Leak Issues
problems.rootCause: The N55 is generally reliable, but it's not without issues. The water pump, thermostat housing, and coolant pipes can develop leaks. Oil leaks from the oil filter housing gasket and valve cover gasket are common. VANOS solenoid failure can cause rough running and reduced power.
problems.recommendation: Check coolant levels regularly and inspect for oil leaks around the oil filter housing. A £250–£550 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than the £3,500+ replacement that follows an overheat. Replace VANOS solenoids at the first sign of rough running.

cta.label: Compare N55 engine prices for your F15 35i →

problems.title: F15 Air Suspension System Failure
problems.affectedModels: All F15 variants with rear air suspension (xDrive models, particularly those with optional Dynamic Drive or self-levelling).
problems.rootCause: Not an engine failure, but the most common non-engine complaint on the F15 X5. The air suspension system — air springs, compressor, valve block, and sensors — is prone to failure. The compressor can overheat and fail; air springs can develop leaks; and the system can become unlevel. Replacement costs are significant.
problems.recommendation: Have the air suspension system inspected at every service — a £200–£500 preventative check is dramatically cheaper than the £1,500+ replacement that follows if the compressor fails or the car is left sitting on its bump stops.

cta.label: Compare F15 air suspension repair quotes →

replacementCosts.h2: F15 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are F15 (third-generation X5) only, produced from April 2013 to July 2018. sDrive denotes rear-wheel drive; xDrive denotes all-wheel drive. The F85 X5 M is the high-performance variant with the S63 V8. The xDrive40e is the plug-in hybrid variant with the N20 four-cylinder petrol engine and electric motor. The F16 X6 shares most of these engines but is covered on its own Generation Page.
